summary: Hostname exfiltration beacon to Burp Collaborator on require()
details: On every require()/import of @onum-releases/shared-components, index.js reads os.hostname() and issues an HTTPS GET to a Burp Collaborator subdomain at `shared-components.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com/shared-components`. The hostname is the installer's machine identifier; sending it via DNS + HTTPS to an attacker-controlled collaborator host confirms an exfiltration channel that fingerprints every machine that loads the package. The package's own description labels itself a 'Security PoC placeholder - benign, no runtime payload', but the shipped code unconditionally beacons. The scope `@onum-releases` also resembles a vendor-namespace impersonation, consistent with a dependency-confusion attempt against the Onum organization.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
